title: Fix data persistence and build meal delivery subscription management app

requirements.text: Ensure all backend stable variables use `stable` keyword for Ingredients, Recipes, Customers, Subscriptions, Invoices, and Plans so data persists across canister upgrades and page refreshes.

quotes: Stable storage on the Internet Computer so all records persist across sessions, refreshes, and tab navigation

acceptanceCriteria: All data collections (ingredients, recipes, customers, subscriptions, invoices, plans) are declared as stable variables in main.mo
After a page refresh, all previously saved records are still retrievable
Adding an ingredient and refreshing the page retains the ingredient in the list

requirements.text: Implement full CRUD backend endpoints for Ingredients: createIngredient, getIngredients, updateIngredient, deleteIngredient — ensuring each operation correctly reads from and writes to stable storage.
acceptanceCriteria: createIngredient persists the ingredient and returns the new record
getIngredients returns all stored ingredients after canister restart
updateIngredient modifies the correct record by ID
deleteIngredient removes the record permanently

requirements.text: Implement full CRUD backend endpoints for Customers: createCustomer, getCustomers, updateCustomer, deleteCustomer — with stable storage.

quotes: name, subscription start/end date, delivery days of the week

acceptanceCriteria: All customer records persist across page refreshes
Customer list returns all saved customers on getCustomers call

requirements.text: Implement full CRUD backend endpoints for Subscriptions: createSubscription, getSubscriptions, updateSubscription, deleteSubscription — storing name, subscription start date, end date, and delivery days of the week per subscriber in stable storage.

quotes: name, subscription start/end date, delivery days of the week

acceptanceCriteria: A new subscription with name, start date, end date, and delivery days can be created and retrieved
Subscription records survive page refresh and tab switching
Delivery days of the week are stored as an array of day names or indices

requirements.text: Implement full CRUD backend endpoints for Plans: createPlan, getPlans, updatePlan, deletePlan — with stable storage.
acceptanceCriteria: Plans persist across sessions
getPlans returns all saved plans

requirements.text: Implement full CRUD backend endpoints for Recipes: createRecipe, getRecipes, updateRecipe, deleteRecipe — with stable storage.
acceptanceCriteria: Recipes persist across sessions
getRecipes returns all saved recipes

requirements.text: Implement full CRUD backend endpoints for Invoices: createInvoice, getInvoices, getInvoicesByCustomer — with stable storage.
acceptanceCriteria: Invoices persist across sessions
getInvoices returns all saved invoices

requirements.text: Fix the IngredientForm component so that submitting the form calls the createIngredient or updateIngredient backend mutation and upon success invalidates the ingredients query cache, causing IngredientList to re-render with the latest data.
acceptanceCriteria: Filling out IngredientForm and clicking Save persists the ingredient
After saving, the ingredient immediately appears in the IngredientList without a manual refresh
Editing an existing ingredient updates it in the list

requirements.text: Ensure all React Query hooks in useQueries.ts correctly wire to the stable backend actor methods for ingredients, recipes, customers, subscriptions, invoices, and plans — so data fetched from the canister is displayed consistently across all app tabs (Inventory, Subscriptions, Customers, Menu, Sales, Plans).

quotes: Saved details (records, ingredients) not showing across all tabs

acceptanceCriteria: Navigating between tabs does not lose previously loaded data
Mutations (create/update/delete) trigger query invalidation so all tabs reflect current state
No stale or empty states appear on tab switch after data has been saved

requirements.text: Preserve all existing page tabs and their routing: Dashboard, Subscriptions, Customers, Inventory, Menu, Sales, and Plans — keeping the current navigation structure in Layout.tsx intact.

quotes: keep the current tabs as it is

acceptanceCriteria: All existing navigation links are present and functional
Each tab routes to its corresponding page component
No existing tabs are removed or renamed

requirements.text: Generate a migration.mo that upgrades any existing subscription day records, preserving the tri-state status variant (#delivered, #missed, #pending) and migrating any legacy boolean `delivered` fields during canister upgrade.
acceptanceCriteria: Canister upgrade does not lose existing subscription delivery day records
Legacy boolean `delivered` fields are converted to the tri-state status variant

constraints: Do not remove or rename any existing navigation tabs
All Motoko logic must remain in backend/main.mo as a single actor
Do not edit files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
nonGoals: Adding new tabs or pages beyond what currently exists
Changing the visual theme or design system
